Synthetic notes on sleep hygiene routines

Keeping a consistent wake time anchors the circadian rhythm more strongly
than a consistent bedtime. Choose one wake time and hold it on weekends.

An evening wind-down routine reduces sleep latency: dim lights an hour
before bed, avoid doom-scrolling, and keep the bedroom slightly cool.

If you cannot fall asleep within twenty minutes, get up and do something
quiet in low light until you feel sleepy, then return to bed.

Caffeine after mid-afternoon and alcohol near bedtime both fragment sleep,
even when they do not prevent falling asleep.
